ogTitle: Old Grevillia vs Old Junee: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Old Grevillia and Old Junee.
heading: Old Grevillia vs Old Junee — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Old Grevillia, New South Wales and Old Junee, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Old Junee has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Old Grevillia (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Old Grevillia or Old Junee safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Old Grevillia has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Old Junee scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Old Grevillia scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Old Junee scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Old Grevillia or Old Junee?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Old Junee has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.78% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
